page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m4.2xlarge is a burstable instance built on the x86_64 architecture using KVM virtualization. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz, configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reflect its burstable CPU allocation. Single-core performance is relatively low, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by Passmark CPU and Geekbench software compilation tests. Memory bandwidth remains stable in the middle 50% tier, reaching 58.95 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Database operations and Redis SET benchmarks show average to weak performance, while static web serving throughput reaches 6.57 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU model, this instance offers a cost-effective profile for workloads that experience intermittent spikes in activity rather than sustained high CPU utilization. It is well-suited for development and testing environments, low-traffic web servers, code repositories, and lightweight microservices. It is less suitable for sustained high-performance computing, large-scale database hosting, or real-time data processing due to the CPU credit limitations inherent in burstable instances.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m4.2xlarge is an x86_64 burstable instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor operating at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Operating under KVM virtualization, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates poor single-core performance, but average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities, with static web serving throughput reaching 6.57 GB/sec. Given its burstable CPU allocation, this instance represents a budget-friendly option for workloads with variable resource demands. It is best suited for development environments, low-to-medium traffic web servers, and lightweight background tasks that do not require continuous, high-intensity CPU utilization.
